comment: Phase 1 airport data for the Condor Skopje landscape. Coordinates are WGS-84 decimal degrees; elevations are metres MSL. Runway true headings are geographic (true) bearings.

airports.name: Skopje International
airports.source: Macedonian eAIP AD 2 LWSK; aerodrome chart true bearings and threshold coordinates

runways._alignment_note: LWSK runway centerline + azimuth measured from installed ortho t0402 by Canny+Hough (align_lwsk_runway.py): painted axis 165.07 deg (whole 165), centroid of the asphalt ribbon at UTM (551565.7,4645443.7). Prior .apt heading was 167.16 (from eAIP thresholds), ~2.1 deg off the painted strip; ends rewritten onto the measured axis so the generate_apt.py end-derived heading rounds to 165. Center moved 111 m onto the painted centerline so the aerotow glider spawns on-runway.

airports.name: Stenkovec
airports.source: Aeroklub Skopje VFR manual / airfield chart; ARP and runway-end coordinates. Center re-aligned 2026-06-21b to put the AEROTOW GLIDER spawn on the USED grass-strip centerline. SPAWN CONVENTION (AERO p.16/p.20/p.49, verified on Slovenia2 Novo Mesto/Ptuj/Celje ortho): the glider spawns ON the .apt axis with ZERO lateral offset; the tug spawns St=50 m (for width=50) to one side (left here, flags2=0x100). In-sim the glider was off the RIGHT strip edge and the tug near the LEFT edge => the .apt axis sat ~26 m RIGHT of the used-strip center. Measured from installed ortho t0704.dds: a regular row of 50-m-spaced grass runway markers (the used-strip centerline) and the worn-grass brightness ridge both lie ~29-31 m NW (left of the 121deg heading) of the OLD axis (532162.2/4656444.0). Net fix: shift center 26.5 m NW (perpendicular, az 31deg) to UTM 532175.9/4656466.7; +2.6 m of leftward heading-rounding throw at the 600 m-back spawn nets the glider ~29 m left = on the used-strip center within ~2 m. Heading kept whole-degree 121 (corridor 120.7 / ends 120.5 / markers 121.3 all round to 121). Width kept 50 (aerotow needs it). Ends translated by the same 26.5 m NW so they stay on the new axis.

airports.name: Kumanovo
airports.source: OurAirports / OpenStreetMap aerodrome and runway data; OSM runway tags length=1200 m, width=43 m, ref=12/30
